Off Calendar May-25-2017 Continued To May-26-17 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Defendants Hh Partners Llc And Hai Ky Noodle House Inc
Off Calendar May-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF BLUE YOUNG's Motion For Sanctions Against Defendants H & H Partners LLC And Hai Ky Noodle House Inc - Plaintiff Blue Young's motion for sanctions is denied in its entirety. Defendant H & H Partners, LLC's motion for summary judgment was successful and thus it cannot be considered frivolous. Defendant Hai Ky Noodle House, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment, while unsuccessful, was not frivolous. The asserted factual inconsistencies and discrepancies in Mr. Young's deposition testimony and interrogatory responses provided Hai Ky Noodle House with an arguable basis to seek summary judgment. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjuducation
Off Calendar Apr-04-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NIKOLAUS W. REED, ESQ. of the Law Office of Nikolaus W. Reed (Tel: 415-940-7766) appearing for the plaimtiff Blue Young;
Atty. BRENNAIN J. GARBER, ESQ. of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter (Tel: 510-457-3458) for defendant H & H Partners;
Atty. NORMAN LAFORCE, ESQ. of the Law Offices of Christian Green (Tel: 510-545-2314) appearing for the defendant Hai Ky Noodle House, Inc.

Matter is argued by counsel and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Hai Ky Noodle House's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. Hai Ky owed plaintiff Blue Young a duty to use reasonable care to prevent harm to Mr. Young. (CACI 400-401; Civil Code 1714(a).) Based on the evidence submitted by Mr. Young and drawing all reasonable inferences in his favor, there are triable disputes on both causes of action for negligence and premises liability whether: a) Hai Ky acted negligently by opening the metal doors and b) that negligence was a substantial factor in causing Mr. Young's injuries. The parties do not dispute that Hai Ky had control of the metal doors at the time Mr. Young claims to have fallen. Mr. Young submitted evidence that the metal doors were open at the time of his fall (Young Deposition, 64:12-65:9.) and that the doors have the capability of unlocking from below the sidewalk. (H&H Partners Responses to Special Interrogatories Set Two, No. 10 attached as Ex. 4 to Reed Declaration). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Kelley DeYoung, CSR #6780, e-mail: kelleycsr@aol.com (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NIKOLAUS W. REED, ESQ. of the Law Office of Nikolaus W. Reed (Tel: 415-940-7766) appearing for the plaimtiff Blue Young;
Atty. BRENNAIN J. GARBER, ESQ. of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter (Tel: 510-457-3458) for defendant H & H Partners;
Atty. NORMAN LAFORCE, ESQ. of the Law Offices of Christian Green (Tel: 510-545-2314) appearing for the defendant Hai Ky Noodle House, Inc.

Matter is argued by counsel and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant H&H Partners, LLC's motion for summary judgment is granted. Assuming the truth of all facts (as opposed to the characterization of those facts) submitted by plaintiff Blue Young in his opposition to H&H's separate statement and his own statement, as a matter of law H&H did not owe a duty to Mr. Young to safely operate the metal doors nor did H&H Partners owe a duty to Mr. Young to make the metal doors safer by installing a buzzer or flashing lights to alert pedestrians. To establish liability on a landlord for a third party's injury due to a dangerous condition, "a plaintiff must show that the landlord had actual knowledge of the dangerous condition in question, plus the right and ability to cure the condition." (Mata v. Mata (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 1121, 1131-32.) H&H presented evidence that it had no notice that the metal doors would be suddenly opened (Material Fact 7; Ha Declaration, 6 and 7), and Mr. Young has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue.
